title: Minecraft
fun_fact: The all-time best-selling game ever — more than GTA V, Tetris, and Wii Sports combined on most measures.

title: Grand Theft Auto V
fun_fact: Still sells roughly 5–7 million copies per year, 12 years after launch.

title: Terraria
fun_fact: A $10 indie game made by one developer has outsold most AAA franchises in history.

title: Stardew Valley
fun_fact: Created by a single developer over four years. Has outsold Cyberpunk 2077 and Elden Ring.

title: PUBG: Battlegrounds
fun_fact: Still holds the all-time Steam peak player record — set in January 2018 — more than 7 years later.

title: Elden Ring
fun_fact: Its DLC Shadow of the Erdtree pushed peak concurrent players to 953k — higher than launch.

title: Cyberpunk 2077
fun_fact: Launched so broken it was pulled from the PS Store. Sold 30M anyway and became one of Steam's biggest peaks ever.

title: Palworld
fun_fact: Hit 2.1M concurrent Steam players in its first week — beating Elden Ring's all-time record in days.

title: Black Myth: Wukong
fun_fact: The first Chinese-developed AAA to crack the global top 50 best-sellers ever. Sold 10M copies in 3 days.

title: Hogwarts Legacy
fun_fact: Best-selling game of 2023 globally. Outsold every mainline Harry Potter game combined.

title: Red Dead Redemption 2
fun_fact: Has an incredibly low Steam peak despite massive total sales — most players are on console.

title: The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt
fun_fact: Sold 50 million copies after 10 years — more than every Elder Scrolls game ever made combined.

title: Baldur's Gate 3
fun_fact: Swept every major Game of the Year award in 2023. Its peak Steam player count rivals Cyberpunk 2077.

title: God of War (2018)
fun_fact: Released on PS4 in 2018, it still regularly outsells new releases on PSN charts years later.

title: God of War Ragnarök
fun_fact: Sold 5 million copies in its first week — the fastest-selling PlayStation Studios game ever at launch.

title: Hades
fun_fact: First game ever to win the Hugo Award for Best Video Game. Scored 93 on Metacritic on a $25 indie budget.

title: Disco Elysium
fun_fact: Scored a 97 on Metacritic — tied with GTA V and Red Dead 2 — but has fewer Steam players than most indie games.

title: Hollow Knight
fun_fact: Developed by 3 people for around $57,000 AUD. Now one of the most critically acclaimed games ever made.

title: Celeste
fun_fact: 94 on Metacritic — same score as God of War 2018 — on a budget of essentially zero.

title: Valheim
fun_fact: Developed by 5 people. Sold 10M copies in its first month in Early Access.

title: Monster Hunter: World
fun_fact: Capcom's best-selling game ever — and it came out after 14 years of Monster Hunter being a niche franchise.

title: Dark Souls III
fun_fact: Sold faster than any other game in the Souls series at launch, despite being the 'final' entry.

title: Sekiro: Shadows Die Twice
fun_fact: 2019 Game of the Year at The Game Awards — but has fewer Steam players than Stardew Valley.

title: ARK: Survival Evolved
fun_fact: Reviewed poorly (70 Metacritic) yet sold 34M copies — more than Elden Ring, Sekiro, and Dark Souls combined.

title: Rust
fun_fact: 69 on Metacritic. Still sold 28M copies — the same as Black Myth: Wukong.

title: Apex Legends
fun_fact: Free-to-play, so sales don't apply — but it has generated over $2 billion in lifetime revenue.

title: Fortnite
fun_fact: Not on Steam at all — so its Steam concurrent player count is literally zero. Has 350M+ registered players.

title: Among Us
fun_fact: Was released in 2018 and almost abandoned. Two years later it hit 500M players worldwide after a viral TikTok moment.

title: Helldivers 2
fun_fact: Sold 12M in its first 12 weeks — Sony's fastest-selling game ever at the time.

title: Diablo IV
fun_fact: Fastest Blizzard game to $1 billion in revenue. Hit that milestone in its first 5 days.

title: Starfield
fun_fact: Bethesda's most-played launch ever with 330K Steam peak — but critically considered a disappointment.

title: Sons of the Forest
fun_fact: Sold 2M copies in its first 24 hours — a sequel to a game most people have never heard of.

title: Lethal Company
fun_fact: Made by one person, sold for $10, and hit 170K concurrent Steam players. No Metacritic score because it launched in Early Access.

title: Dead Cells
fun_fact: 89 on Metacritic and 6M sales — but a lower Steam peak than most people would expect.

title: Monster Hunter Wilds
fun_fact: Broke the record for peak concurrent players at a Monster Hunter launch on day one — hitting 1.3M on Steam.

title: Doom Eternal
fun_fact: 88 on Metacritic, one of the best FPS games ever made — but sold far fewer copies than ARK: Survival Evolved.

title: Destiny 2
fun_fact: Free to play, so sales don't apply — but it has generated over $4 billion in total revenue.

title: Overwatch
fun_fact: Not on Steam — sold 50M copies through Battle.net before going free-to-play as Overwatch 2.

title: Overwatch 2
fun_fact: Has one of the lowest user scores in gaming history (1.6) despite 35M players in its first month.

title: Marvel Rivals
fun_fact: Hit 40M players in its first 30 days — the fastest-growing hero shooter launch ever.

title: Counter-Strike 2
fun_fact: CS:GO (its predecessor) holds one of Steam's all-time CCU records. CS2 continues that legacy with 1.8M peak.

title: Path of Exile 2
fun_fact: Free to play in Early Access and still hit 577K concurrent Steam players at launch.

title: The Last of Us Part I
fun_fact: 95 on Metacritic and 26M sales — but the PC version had a disastrous launch that tanked Steam reviews.

title: Outer Wilds
fun_fact: Considered by many to be one of the greatest games ever made. Only 1.5M sales.

title: Hi-Fi Rush
fun_fact: Was a surprise Xbox Game Pass shadow drop. Won multiple awards. Publisher Tango Gameworks was shut down by Microsoft 3 months later.

title: Dave the Diver
fun_fact: 90 on Metacritic — higher than ARK, Cyberpunk, and Palworld — and somehow nobody was expecting it.

title: Lies of P
fun_fact: A Korean Souls-like based on Pinocchio. 80 on Metacritic and its Steam peak rivals God of War on PC.

title: Alan Wake 2
fun_fact: 89 on Metacritic, praised as one of 2023's best — but its PC player count is lower than most expect for a AAA.

title: Cocoon
fun_fact: Made by a single developer. 90 on Metacritic. Fewer Steam players at peak than a small Discord server.

title: Phasmophobia
fun_fact: Developed by one person. Sold 22M copies from Early Access — more than Doom Eternal, Hades, and Dead Cells combined.

title: Balatro
fun_fact: A poker-themed roguelike made by one developer that was nominated for Game of the Year at The Game Awards 2024.

title: Ghost of Tsushima
fun_fact: A PS4 exclusive that became Sony's biggest PC launch when it finally came to Steam in 2024.

title: Forza Horizon 5
fun_fact: 92 on Metacritic and 35M players — but available on Game Pass, so most players never paid for it.

title: Fall Guys
fun_fact: Sold 7M copies in its first month when paid, then went free-to-play. Total players hit 100M after the switch.

title: Vampire Survivors
fun_fact: Originally sold for $2.99. Made by one developer. 86 on Metacritic — same as PUBG and Cyberpunk 2077.

title: Dota 2
fun_fact: Free to play. Still one of the top 3 games on Steam by concurrent players more than 12 years after launch.

title: Warframe
fun_fact: Free to play and now 12 years old — still generates hundreds of millions in revenue annually with zero major marketing spend.

title: FIFA 23 / EA Sports FC 24
fun_fact: 79 on Metacritic. 4.4 user score. Still one of the best-selling games of its release year every single year.

title: The Forest
fun_fact: 72 on Metacritic and 30M sales — more than Cyberpunk 2077. Players clearly ignored the critics.

title: Project Zomboid
fun_fact: Has been in Early Access since 2013 — over 12 years. Still has one of the highest user scores on Steam.

title: Garry's Mod
fun_fact: A $10 sandbox game from 2006 has sold 25.5M copies — more than any FromSoftware game ever made.

title: Horizon Zero Dawn
fun_fact: Sold 24M copies — more than most long-running franchises achieve across multiple entries.

title: Horizon Forbidden West
fun_fact: 88 on Metacritic but sold far less than its predecessor despite being a bigger game.

title: Marvel's Spider-Man
fun_fact: The best-selling superhero game ever made with 33M copies sold.

title: Marvel's Spider-Man 2
fun_fact: Sold 11M copies in just 3 months — the fastest-selling PlayStation Studios game.

title: Spider-Man: Miles Morales
fun_fact: A shorter spin-off that still sold over 10M — proving Miles can carry a game solo.

title: Uncharted 4: A Thief's End
fun_fact: 37M copies sold but only 10K peak on Steam — it's overwhelmingly a PlayStation game.

title: Death Stranding
fun_fact: Kojima's divisive post-Konami debut sold 15M copies despite mixed fan reception.

title: Final Fantasy VII Remake
fun_fact: A remake of a 1997 game that took 5 years to develop and only covers the first act of the original.

title: Final Fantasy XVI
fun_fact: Only 3.5M sales despite being a mainline Final Fantasy — the series' commercial peak is long past.

title: Final Fantasy XV
fun_fact: Took 10 years to develop across two console generations. Sold 10M copies.

title: Resident Evil Village
fun_fact: Lady Dimitrescu broke the internet and drove this to 9M sales.

title: Resident Evil 4 Remake
fun_fact: 93 on Metacritic — matching the original's legendary status 18 years later.

title: Resident Evil 2 Remake
fun_fact: Outsold the 1998 original within months. 13M copies of a remake of a 20-year-old game.

title: Resident Evil 7: Biohazard
fun_fact: Revived the franchise by going first-person. Low Steam peak despite 13M total sales.

title: Devil May Cry 5
fun_fact: Brought the series back to form after the controversial DmC reboot.

title: Mass Effect Legendary Edition
fun_fact: Three massive RPGs remastered in one package — over 80 hours to complete.

title: Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order
fun_fact: Proved single-player Star Wars games could sell 20M without multiplayer or microtransactions.

title: Star Wars Jedi: Survivor
fun_fact: Higher Metacritic than its predecessor but sold far fewer copies.

title: Assassin's Creed Valhalla
fun_fact: 20M sales but a 5.6 user score — one of the biggest critic-fan divides in AC history.

title: Assassin's Creed Odyssey
fun_fact: So massive that 100% completion takes over 130 hours.

title: Assassin's Creed Mirage
fun_fact: A deliberate return to shorter, classic AC gameplay after years of bloated RPGs.

title: Far Cry 5
fun_fact: 25M copies sold on a 78 Metacritic — Far Cry sells despite middling reviews.

title: Far Cry 6
fun_fact: Featured Giancarlo Esposito as the villain but received the lowest Metacritic in the mainline series.

title: Halo Infinite
fun_fact: The multiplayer was F2P with 272K Steam peak, but the player base collapsed within months.

title: Gears 5
fun_fact: Excellent reviews but low Steam numbers — Xbox exclusive games struggle on PC.

title: Sea of Thieves
fun_fact: Launched to a 69 Metacritic but grew to 25M players — a rare live-service success story.

title: Titanfall 2
fun_fact: 89 Metacritic and one of the best FPS campaigns ever — but EA launched it between Battlefield and CoD.

title: Borderlands 3
fun_fact: Sold 18M copies in under 2 years despite a 5.3 user score.

title: Dying Light 2
fun_fact: 275K peak on Steam — one of the highest for a non-franchise sequel.

title: Hitman 3
fun_fact: The World of Assassination trilogy is widely considered the peak of modern stealth gaming.

title: Control
fun_fact: Won multiple GOTY awards but only sold 3M — a cult classic in every sense.

title: Deathloop
fun_fact: 88 Metacritic but only 2M sales — one of the biggest gaps between critical and commercial success.

title: Returnal
fun_fact: A $70 roguelike that divided players on pricing but earned 86 on Metacritic.

title: Ratchet & Clank: Rift Apart
fun_fact: One of the first true PS5 showcases — stunning visuals but modest sales.

title: Astro Bot
fun_fact: 94 on Metacritic — the highest-rated PS5 exclusive. Won GOTY 2024 at The Game Awards.

title: It Takes Two
fun_fact: Won GOTY 2021 and sold 16M — proving co-op-only games can be massive hits.

title: A Way Out
fun_fact: Director Josef Fares famously said 'F*** the Oscars' at The Game Awards before its launch.

title: Atomic Heart
fun_fact: A Russian-developed alt-history shooter that generated massive controversy and massive sales.

title: Dead Space Remake
fun_fact: 89 Metacritic — the remake matched the legendary original's quality, but EA shut down the studio anyway.

title: Gotham Knights
fun_fact: 61 Metacritic — one of the lowest-rated DC games ever despite years of hype.

title: Suicide Squad: Kill the Justice League
fun_fact: Rocksteady's follow-up to the Arkham trilogy scored 60 on Metacritic. A spectacular fall from grace.

title: Skull and Bones
fun_fact: Took 11 years to develop and scored 59 on Metacritic. Cost Ubisoft an estimated $200M.

title: Redfall
fun_fact: Arkane Austin's final game before closure. 55 Metacritic — the studio's worst-ever score.

title: Anthem
fun_fact: BioWare's biggest commercial failure. EA pulled the plug on its planned overhaul in 2021.

title: Marvel's Avengers
fun_fact: Servers shut down in 2024. Lost Square Enix an estimated $200M despite the Marvel brand.

title: Undertale
fun_fact: Made by one person. 92 on Metacritic. Changed how people think about RPG combat forever.

title: Cuphead
fun_fact: Hand-animated in 1930s cartoon style. Took 7 years to make. Sold 12M copies.

title: Slay the Spire
fun_fact: Created the deckbuilder roguelike genre. 7M copies sold of a game made by 2 people.

title: Into the Breach
fun_fact: From the makers of FTL. 89 Metacritic. Tiny player count but enormous critical acclaim.

title: FTL: Faster Than Light
fun_fact: Kickstarted for $200K in 2012. Helped launch the entire roguelike indie boom.

title: The Binding of Isaac: Rebirth
fun_fact: Has received continuous updates for over 10 years. Players average 50+ hours.

title: Risk of Rain 2
fun_fact: Jumped from 2D to 3D and somehow improved on the original in every way.

title: Subnautica
fun_fact: A survival game where the scariest enemy is the ocean itself. 8M copies sold.

title: Satisfactory
fun_fact: Spent 6 years in Early Access. Players average 80 hours — and many hit 500+.

title: Factorio
fun_fact: One of the highest-rated games on Steam with 98% positive reviews. Notoriously addictive.

title: RimWorld
fun_fact: A colony sim with emergent storytelling so wild that players write novels about their colonies.

title: Ori and the Blind Forest
fun_fact: An Xbox exclusive so beautiful it made grown adults cry. 5M sales.

title: Ori and the Will of the Wisps
fun_fact: Improved on the original in every way. 90 on Metacritic.

title: Tunic
fun_fact: A Zelda-inspired game with a secret language players had to collectively decode.

title: Sifu
fun_fact: Your character ages every time they die. Start as a 20-year-old, end as an elderly martial artist.

title: Inscryption
fun_fact: Starts as a card game, becomes something completely different. Multiple Game of the Year awards.

title: Cult of the Lamb
fun_fact: Run a cult as an adorable lamb. 5M sales. Devolver Digital's biggest hit ever.

title: Neon White
fun_fact: 88 Metacritic but only 1M sales — beloved by critics but under-discovered by players.

title: Pizza Tower
fun_fact: Made by one person. 88 Metacritic. Compared to Wario Land but somehow even more chaotic.

title: Ultrakill
fun_fact: An Early Access retro FPS with one of the highest user scores on Steam. Coin-shotgun combo.

title: Deep Rock Galactic
fun_fact: Rock and Stone! A co-op mining FPS with one of gaming's most wholesome communities.

title: Darkest Dungeon
fun_fact: Made stress and mental health a core game mechanic. 5M copies sold.

title: Hades II
fun_fact: Hit 100K concurrent Steam players in Early Access — before it was even finished.

title: Spiritfarer
fun_fact: A cozy management game about dying — somehow one of the most touching games ever made.

title: The Witness
fun_fact: Made by Jonathan Blow (of Braid fame). Over 600 puzzles. No dialogue.

title: Limbo
fun_fact: 90 Metacritic for a silent black-and-white puzzle game that takes 3 hours to finish.

title: Inside
fun_fact: 93 on Metacritic — higher than most AAA games. Entire playthrough is under 4 hours.

title: Little Nightmares
fun_fact: 6M sales for a 4-hour horror platformer with no dialogue.

title: Little Nightmares II
fun_fact: The sequel was praised but sold half as many copies as the original.

title: Firewatch
fun_fact: A 4-hour walking simulator that sparked a massive debate about what counts as a game.

title: What Remains of Edith Finch
fun_fact: 89 Metacritic for a 2-hour game. Won BAFTA Game of the Year.

title: Journey
fun_fact: 92 Metacritic. 2 hours long. No words. The first game ever nominated for a Grammy.

title: Katana ZERO
fun_fact: A neo-noir action game where you can deflect bullets with a katana.

title: Hotline Miami
fun_fact: Ultra-violent, neon-soaked, and made by 2 people. Spawned an entire aesthetic movement.

title: Hotline Miami 2: Wrong Number
fun_fact: Banned in Australia for extreme violence. Lower Metacritic than the original.

title: Noita
fun_fact: Every pixel is physically simulated. You can burn, melt, or dissolve the entire world.

title: Rain World
fun_fact: 68 Metacritic at launch, now considered a masterpiece. The DLC pushed Steam reviews to Overwhelmingly Positive.

title: Shovel Knight
fun_fact: A Kickstarter success that became one of the most beloved indie games of all time. 90 Metacritic.

title: Return of the Obra Dinn
fun_fact: Made entirely by one person using 1-bit graphics. Won IGF Grand Prize and multiple GOTY awards.

title: Unpacking
fun_fact: A game about unpacking boxes. No dialogue, no combat. 84 Metacritic.

title: Braid
fun_fact: Jonathan Blow spent $200K of his own money making it. Helped launch the entire indie game movement.

title: Oxenfree
fun_fact: Pioneered a real-time dialogue system that many games have since copied.

title: The Messenger
fun_fact: Starts as an 8-bit platformer, then transforms into a 16-bit Metroidvania halfway through.

title: CrossCode
fun_fact: A retro-styled ARPG with 35+ hours of content. Took 7 years to develop.

title: Superliminal
fun_fact: Perspective-based puzzles — pick up a small object and it becomes huge. A real mind-bender.

title: A Short Hike
fun_fact: A 90-minute game about climbing a mountain. 83 Metacritic. Costs $8.

title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim
fun_fact: Re-released on 14 different platforms. 60M copies sold. Todd Howard's magnum opus.

title: Fallout 4
fun_fact: 472K Steam peak at launch. TV show in 2024 brought so many players back that it set a new peak.

title: Fallout: New Vegas
fun_fact: Obsidian made it in 18 months. Fans consider it the best Fallout ever — Bethesda hasn't topped it.

title: Portal 2
fun_fact: 95 on Metacritic — one of the highest-rated games of all time. The co-op ruined friendships.

title: Half-Life 2
fun_fact: 96 Metacritic. Revolutionized gaming physics. Fans have waited 20 years for a sequel.

title: Left 4 Dead 2
fun_fact: Still has 15-30K concurrent players in 2025 — over 15 years after launch.

title: Team Fortress 2
fun_fact: Free-to-play since 2011. Its hat economy is worth millions. Valve barely updates it anymore.

title: BioShock Infinite
fun_fact: 94 Metacritic. The ending is still debated over a decade later.

title: Civilization VI
fun_fact: 12M sales but a 6.2 user score — fans are divided on whether it's better than Civ V.

title: Civilization V
fun_fact: 90 Metacritic. Average playtime is 50 hours but many players have 1,000+.

title: XCOM 2
fun_fact: One of the hardest strategy games ever. Ironman mode runs can end with a single bad decision.

title: Total War: Warhammer III
fun_fact: 167K peak on Steam. The combined map with all three games is the biggest Total War map ever.

title: Cities: Skylines
fun_fact: Killed SimCity. 12M copies sold. The modding community is enormous.

title: Divinity: Original Sin 2
fun_fact: 93 Metacritic — tied with the greatest RPGs of all time. Directly inspired Baldur's Gate 3.

title: Dragon's Dogma 2
fun_fact: 232K peak on Steam but a 5.6 user score — controversial microtransactions tanked reception.

title: Dark Souls
fun_fact: Created an entire genre named after it. 'Soulslike' is now a standard game category.

title: Dark Souls II
fun_fact: The 'worst' Dark Souls is still 87 on Metacritic. Directed by a different team than 1 and 3.

title: Bloodborne
fun_fact: 92 Metacritic. Still a PS4 exclusive 10 years later. Fans have begged for a PC port since launch.

title: NieR: Automata
fun_fact: You have to beat the game 3 times to see the real ending. 8M copies sold.

title: Persona 5 Royal
fun_fact: 95 Metacritic. 103 hours average playtime. One of the greatest JRPGs ever made.

title: Dragon Quest XI S
fun_fact: 92 on Metacritic. 57-hour average. Revived Western interest in the Dragon Quest franchise.

title: Rocket League
fun_fact: Went F2P in 2020. Soccer with rocket cars — simple concept, infinite skill ceiling.

title: Rainbow Six Siege
fun_fact: Almost died at launch. Now 85M players and one of the biggest esports in the world.

title: Escape from Tarkov
fun_fact: No Metacritic score, no Steam release, and still sold 7M copies. The hardcore shooter king.

title: Hunt: Showdown
fun_fact: A PvPvE horror shooter with some of the best sound design in gaming.

title: Dead by Daylight
fun_fact: 71 Metacritic yet 15M sales and still growing. Licensed killers from every horror franchise.

title: The Finals
fun_fact: 265K peak players at launch — fully destructible environments set it apart from other shooters.

title: Valorant
fun_fact: Not on Steam. 30M+ monthly players. Riot's answer to CS:GO became a massive esport.

title: League of Legends
fun_fact: The most-played PC game in the world for over a decade. 180M+ monthly players.

title: Lost Ark
fun_fact: Hit 1.3M peak on Steam at Western launch — the second-highest ever at the time.

title: New World
fun_fact: 914K peak on Steam at launch — then lost 90% of players within 3 months.

title: Final Fantasy XIV
fun_fact: Launched as a disaster in 2010 (49 Metacritic), rebooted as A Realm Reborn and became the best MMO ever.

title: Genshin Impact
fun_fact: Generated over $5 billion in revenue. Not on Steam. Changed the gacha game industry forever.

title: Honkai: Star Rail
fun_fact: Generated $1B+ in its first year. Made by the same studio as Genshin Impact.

title: NBA 2K24
fun_fact: 1.2 user score — one of the lowest ever — yet still sold 8M copies. Sports games sell no matter what.

title: Gran Turismo 7
fun_fact: 87 Metacritic but a 2.0 user score — monetization backlash tanked player reviews.

title: Age of Empires IV
fun_fact: Brought back the classic RTS franchise after 16 years. 74K Steam peak.

title: Crusader Kings III
fun_fact: A strategy game where you can seduce the Pope. 91 Metacritic. 100K Steam peak.

title: Stellaris
fun_fact: Paradox's space 4X that lets you commit interstellar genocide or uplift primitive species.

title: Anno 1800
fun_fact: Was an Epic exclusive that still sold 3M — proving the Anno franchise has dedicated fans.

title: Frostpunk
fun_fact: A city builder where child labor and organ harvesting are legitimate survival strategies.

title: Manor Lords
fun_fact: Made by one person. 173K peak on Steam in Early Access. 3M copies in 3 weeks.

title: Amnesia: The Dark Descent
fun_fact: Defined the modern horror game genre. PewDiePie's Let's Play of it helped launch YouTube gaming.

title: Outlast
fun_fact: No weapons, just a night-vision camcorder. 4M sales from pure terror.

title: SOMA
fun_fact: From the makers of Amnesia. Asks the question: what does it mean to be human?

title: Alien: Isolation
fun_fact: The Alien AI learns your hiding patterns. 5M sales. Considered the best Alien game ever.

title: Doom (2016)
fun_fact: Revived the Doom franchise with a game about being too angry to die.

title: Prey (2017)
fun_fact: Critically acclaimed but commercially underperformed. A cult classic in the immersive sim genre.

title: Dishonored 2
fun_fact: The Clockwork Mansion level is considered one of the greatest levels in gaming history.

title: Doom (1993)
fun_fact: Created the FPS genre. Has been ported to everything — calculators, pregnancy tests, ATMs.

title: Halo: The Master Chief Collection
fun_fact: 6 Halo games in one package. 161K Steam peak when it finally came to PC.

title: Metal Gear Solid V: The Phantom Pain
fun_fact: 95 Metacritic. Kojima's final Metal Gear. Famously unfinished due to his fallout with Konami.

title: Resident Evil 3 Remake
fun_fact: 7M sales despite being the shortest RE remake at just 6 hours.

title: Nioh 2
fun_fact: Team Ninja's answer to Dark Souls — harder, faster, and with 60 hours of content.

title: Biomutant
fun_fact: 56K Steam peak from hype alone. 62 Metacritic. A cautionary tale of marketing vs reality.

title: Warhammer 40,000: Space Marine 2
fun_fact: 226K Steam peak. Surprised everyone by being genuinely great.

title: The Outer Worlds
fun_fact: Obsidian's 'Fallout in space.' 5M copies. Shorter than expected but well-loved.

title: No Man's Sky
fun_fact: Launched as a disaster (61 Metacritic). Years of free updates turned it into one of gaming's greatest redemption stories.

title: Grounded
fun_fact: Honey I Shrunk the Kids as a survival game. 15M players through Game Pass.

title: Warhammer: Vermintide 2
fun_fact: Left 4 Dead meets Warhammer Fantasy. 5M copies sold.

title: V Rising
fun_fact: Build a vampire castle, hunt for blood. 150K Steam peak. 4M copies sold.

title: Enshrouded
fun_fact: 161K peak players in Early Access. Valheim-like survival with RPG combat.

title: Remnant 2
fun_fact: 88K Steam peak. A Soulslike shooter that no one expected to be this good.

title: Wo Long: Fallen Dynasty
fun_fact: Team Ninja's Three Kingdoms Soulslike. 3M copies in its first month.

title: Armored Core VI: Fires of Rubicon
fun_fact: FromSoftware's return to Armored Core after 10 years. 149K peak — more than any previous AC.

title: Immortals Fenyx Rising
fun_fact: Ubisoft's Breath of the Wild clone. Decent reviews but lost in the shuffle.

title: Watch Dogs 2
fun_fact: Universally considered better than the first Watch Dogs but sold fewer copies.

title: Tiny Tina's Wonderlands
fun_fact: Borderlands meets D&D. Fun concept but DLC was universally panned.

title: Ghostwire: Tokyo
fun_fact: Tango Gameworks' supernatural action game set in an empty Tokyo.

title: Sable
fun_fact: Moebius-inspired open world. Beautiful but buggy at launch.

title: Pentiment
fun_fact: 87 Metacritic for a medieval detective game by Obsidian. Incredibly niche but acclaimed.

title: Psychonauts 2
fun_fact: Tim Schafer's long-awaited sequel. 87 Metacritic. Took 6 years to make.

title: Kena: Bridge of Spirits
fun_fact: Made by a 15-person team. Looks like a Pixar movie. 80 on Metacritic.

title: Dying Light
fun_fact: 74 Metacritic but 30M sales. Zombie parkour sells.

title: Warzone
fun_fact: F2P with 100M+ players. Helped Call of Duty dominate the battle royale space.

title: Call of Duty: Modern Warfare II (2022)
fun_fact: 30M copies sold in 10 days. 75 Metacritic. 3.4 user score. CoD sells regardless of quality.

title: Raft
fun_fact: Survive on a raft in the ocean. Started as a student project. 5M copies sold.

title: 7 Days to Die
fun_fact: In Early Access since 2013 — over 12 years. Still sold 15M copies.

title: Don't Starve Together
fun_fact: Klei Entertainment's gothic survival. 8M copies and still getting major updates.

title: Civilization VII
fun_fact: 170K Steam peak at launch but a divisive 4.0 user score — fans debated its new Age system.

title: Metaphor: ReFantazio
fun_fact: 94 Metacritic — from the Persona team. The highest-rated JRPG of 2024.

title: Like a Dragon: Infinite Wealth
fun_fact: 92 Metacritic. The Yakuza series reinvented as a JRPG and it's better than ever.

title: Tekken 8
fun_fact: 90 Metacritic but a 5.5 user score — battle pass monetization infuriated the community.

title: Street Fighter 6
fun_fact: Revived the franchise after Street Fighter V's rough launch. 92 Metacritic.

title: Mortal Kombat 1
fun_fact: The timeline reboot. 84 Metacritic. Switch version was infamously terrible.

title: Guilty Gear Strive
fun_fact: Arc System Works made a fighting game that looks like an anime. 3M copies sold.

title: MultiVersus
fun_fact: Batman vs Bugs Bunny. 145K peak. 63 Metacritic. A spectacular failure after a promising beta.

title: Dragon Ball FighterZ
fun_fact: 10M copies — the best-selling Dragon Ball game ever. Captures the anime perfectly.

title: Wuthering Waves
fun_fact: Genshin Impact's main competitor. 69 Metacritic at launch but improved rapidly.

title: Stellar Blade
fun_fact: A PS5 exclusive that generated massive debate about character design.

title: Still Wakes the Deep
fun_fact: An oil rig horror game by the Dear Esther team. 5 hours of pure atmosphere.

title: Animal Well
fun_fact: 91 Metacritic. Made by one person. Has secret puzzles no one has solved yet.

title: Pacific Drive
fun_fact: A survival game where your car is your home, your weapon, and your best friend.

title: Satisfactory 1.0
fun_fact: Spent 6 years in Early Access before 1.0 launch. Players average 80+ hours.

title: Titanfall
fun_fact: Defined the wallrunning FPS movement but servers were shut down due to hacking.

title: Splatoon 3
fun_fact: Nintendo's shooter franchise. 12M copies on Switch alone.

title: Mario Kart 8 Deluxe
fun_fact: 64M copies — the best-selling Switch game ever. More than most entire franchises.

title: The Legend of Zelda: Tears of the Kingdom
fun_fact: 96 Metacritic — tied for the highest-rated game of the 2020s.

title: The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild
fun_fact: 97 Metacritic. Redefined open-world game design. Every game since has been compared to it.

title: Super Smash Bros. Ultimate
fun_fact: 34M copies. 89 playable characters. The ultimate crossover event in gaming history.

title: Animal Crossing: New Horizons
fun_fact: 44M copies — launched during COVID lockdowns and became a global cultural phenomenon.

title: Pokemon Legends: Arceus
fun_fact: Finally gave Pokemon fans the open-world game they'd been requesting for 20 years.

title: Pokemon Scarlet & Violet
fun_fact: 25M sales despite a 72 Metacritic and 2.8 user score. Pokemon is critic-proof.

title: Fire Emblem: Three Houses
fun_fact: Turned Fire Emblem from niche to mainstream. 4M copies and a massive fan community.

title: Xenoblade Chronicles 3
fun_fact: 89 Metacritic. 65-hour main story. One of the most ambitious JRPGs ever made.

title: Metroid Dread
fun_fact: The first new 2D Metroid in 19 years. Sold 3M copies — a franchise record.

title: Bayonetta 3
fun_fact: 86 Metacritic but controversy over voice actor recasting tanked the user score.

title: Pikmin 4
fun_fact: The best-selling Pikmin game ever. Finally made the franchise mainstream.

title: Contra: Operation Galuga
fun_fact: A modern revival of the classic run-and-gun franchise.

title: Diablo III
fun_fact: 30M copies sold but a 3.8 user score at launch — the real-money auction house disaster.

title: Diablo II: Resurrected
fun_fact: A remaster of one of the greatest games ever. Blizzard somehow fumbled the launch.

title: Overcooked! 2
fun_fact: The game most likely to end friendships and relationships. Chaotic co-op cooking.

title: Stray
fun_fact: You play as a cat. That's it. That's why it sold 3M copies.

title: PowerWash Simulator
fun_fact: A game about pressure washing. 5M copies sold. Somehow incredibly relaxing.

title: Paladin's Oath
fun_fact: A tactics RPG inspired by Final Fantasy Tactics that flew under the radar.

title: Elden Ring: Shadow of the Erdtree
fun_fact: 94 Metacritic but 6.5 user score — fans review-bombed it for being too hard.

title: Wukong: Black Myth DLC
fun_fact: The highly anticipated expansion to one of 2024's biggest games.

title: Indiana Jones and the Great Circle
fun_fact: MachineGames (Wolfenstein devs) made an Indiana Jones game. 85 Metacritic.

title: Path of Exile
fun_fact: Free-to-play. Considered the true spiritual successor to Diablo 2 by hardcore ARPG fans.

title: Wargroove
fun_fact: A spiritual successor to Advance Wars made by an indie studio.

title: Chants of Sennaar
fun_fact: Learn fictional languages to solve puzzles. One of the most unique games of 2023.

title: Voices of the Void
fun_fact: A free indie horror game about a scientist alone at a satellite array. Cult following.

title: 1000xRESIST
fun_fact: 90 Metacritic. One of the most critically acclaimed indie games of 2024.
